---
title: "Cite"
page-layout: full
---

::: {.hero}
::: {.container}
# How to Cite DPmixGPD

If you use DPmixGPD in academic work, reports, or software, please cite it appropriately. Clear citation helps track usage, justify maintenance, and support continued development.

[GitHub Repository](https://github.com/arnabaich96/DPmixGPD){.btn .btn-primary}
[Open Discussions](https://github.com/arnabaich96/DPmixGPD/discussions){.btn .btn-outline-light}
:::
:::

::: {.section}
::: {.container}

## Software citation (current)

Until a formal software DOI is assigned, please cite the software repository directly. A standard citation format is:

::: {.cardx}
**DPmixGPD: Bayesian Semiparametric Modeling for Heavy-Tailed Data**  
Arnab Aich.  
GitHub repository: https://github.com/arnabaich96/DPmixGPD
:::

For BibTeX users:

::: {.cardx}
@software{DPmixGPD,
author = {Aich, Arnab},
title = {DPmixGPD: Bayesian Semiparametric Modeling for Heavy-Tailed Data},
year = {2025},
url = {https://github.com/arnabaich96/DPmixGPD}
,
note = {R package}
} 

:::

## Methodological citation

If your work relies on the methodological ideas implemented in DPmixGPD (Dirichlet-process mixtures, extreme-value tail modeling, causal or survival extensions), please also cite the relevant methodological references described in the accompanying papers or documentation.

When a peer-reviewed article becomes available, this page will be updated with the preferred citation.

## Versioning and reproducibility

For reproducibility:
- Report the package version you used.
- Record your R version and platform.
- If possible, include session information in supplementary materials.

This helps others reproduce results and interpret differences across versions.

## Industrial and applied use

For applied or industry-facing work where formal academic citation is not standard, we recommend referencing:
- the package name (DPmixGPD),
- the GitHub repository URL,
- and the version used.

## Updates to citation information

Citation guidance will evolve as:
- a formal software DOI is registered,
- associated papers are published,
- or long-term archival releases are created.

If you are unsure how to cite DPmixGPD for a specific venue, start a Discussion and ask.

[Ask a citation question](https://github.com/arnabaich96/DPmixGPD/discussions){.btn .btn-outline-primary}

:::
:::
